description: Universal-core numeric predicates — FAILING surface (§55.1). On numeric (type=number) fields, min/max/gt/lt/gte/lte each driven past their literal bound → the matching ValidationError tag (MinFailed / MaxFailed / GtFailed / LtFailed / GteFailed / LteFailed) with the threshold/expected payload (§55.9), isValid false per field, compound rollup false.
rationale: §55.1 the six comparison predicates fire numerically on a number-typed cell; §55.9 tags carry threshold (min/max) or expected (gt/lt/gte/lte). Literal args keep this self-contained — cross-field (@cell args) is §55.11 / item 4. ESCALATED DIVERGENCE (min/max string-coercion): on a STRING (type=text) field, min/max ALWAYS fire (NaN comparison) while gt/lt/gte/lte coerce the string correctly — an impl#1 min/max-vs-comparison inconsistency vs the §55.1 'numeric' intent. This case sidesteps it via the canonical numeric-field form (type=number); the string-field inconsistency is flagged to the PA, NOT enshrined here.
